summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing for Sadhna Dhiman coaching site. 5/5 pytest backend tests pass. All 12 required frontend UI/integration checks pass, including strict privacy (phone '7986498916' not in DOM, 'Ma'am' not in DOM), title correctness, sticky nav Calendly window.open intercept, hero CTA window.open, all 6 smooth-scroll nav links, all 8 service cards, testimonials next/prev, FAQ accordion expansion (data-state closed->open), full contact form submit + DB persistence via GET /api/inquiries, contact Calendly button, and footer email/LinkedIn hrefs.
critical_code_review_comments: Info-only: data-testid 'faq-item-{i}' is placed on the AccordionItem wrapper div, not on the trigger button; aria-expanded is null there but data-state toggles closed<->open. Works correctly for users; if a testing framework relies strictly on aria-expanded, consider moving the testid onto AccordionTrigger.
server.py uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI — consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
list_inquiries has no auth/pagination beyond a 1000 doc cap — acceptable for MVP, but should be protected before production.
seed_data_creation: A handful of TEST_UI_* / TEST_User_* inquiries were created in MongoDB db.inquiries during pytest and Playwright runs. They persist harmlessly and can be pruned by name prefix if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is used for all tests. RESEND_API_KEY is a placeholder — inquiry POST returns coach_email_id/reply_email_id = null but still persists. Calendly URL is https://calendly.com/sadhna-dhiman. Frontend uses window.open (not target='_blank' anchors) so intercept window.open before clicking to verify. FAQ testid is on the wrapper div; assert data-state ('closed' vs 'open'), NOT aria-expanded.
